subject: Re: Confusion about parameters and their negative counterparts.
body:
Hi Shintaro,

Sorry for the delay here - I've been away :-)

I've actually never looked at the internals here but it wouldn't surprise
me if the values passed at the CLI have a different convention compared to
the values in the settings files. I'm also not sure about the sanity of the
defaults here, this used to be something that was set in the Warp GUI so
was very visible to users. Do you think there is a bug somewhere where a
value is misinterpreted or are you just trying to understand the internals
of the metadata files?

Cheers,

Alister

On Wed, 14 Aug 2024 at 16:41, Shintaro Aibara <shintaro.aibara@gmail.com>
wrote:

> Dear Alister,
>
> I have a question about two parameters in WarpTools create_settings.
>
> For --exposure, the help mentions it is the "Overall exposure per
> Angstrom^2, use negative value to specify exposure/frame instead". Now,
> when I plonk in the overall exposure (e.g. 60), the value that gets written
> out to the .settings file is "-60" for DosePerAngstromFrame. So WarpTools
> seems to *-1 the value I put in. To me it's confusing that the default
> value for this parameter is "1" which almost makes me think that it's not
> the overall exposure by default?
>
> Likewise eer_ngroups also seems to take the negative of the value I put
> in, so I'm really uncertain if that is expected behaviour or not. I.e.
> whether the .settings files work on the inverse logic of WarpTools.
>
> Hope that makes some sense to you.
>
> Best wishes
> Shintaro
>

> .
>
